requirementCueLines: Bachelor of Arts - Music, Music Theory Option | School of Music | University of Washington
Bachelor of Arts - Music, Music Theory Option

This degree is offered with a major in Music, and is intended for students who wish to emphasize general competence in music within the framework of a liberal arts education. Students must complete a minimum of 180 credits, of which 90 must be taken in departments other than the School of Music, including the Language Skills , Reasoning and Writing in Context , and Areas of Inquiry requirements of the College of Arts and Sciences (see below). Music majors are required to earn a minimum grade of 2.0 in each music course (core and elective) that is to count toward music degree requirements. A GPA of 2.5 in music is required for graduation.
MUSIC 113: Beginning Ear Training ( Autumn ) (1 credit)
MUSIC 119: Theory ( Autumn ) (3 credits)
MUSIC 120: Survey of Music History ( Aut/Win/Spr ) (5 credits) or proficiency exam
MUSIC 201, 202: First-Year Theory I, II ( Winter, Spring ) (3,3) (6 credits)
MUSIC 204, 205: First-Year Ear Training I, II ( Winter, Spring ) (1,1) (2 credits)
MUSIC 203, 301, 302: First-Year Theory III & Second-Year Theory I, II ( Aut,Win,Spr ) (3,3,3) (9 credits)
MUSIC 206, 304, 305: First-Year Ear Training III & Second Year-Ear Training I, II ( Aut,Win,Spr ) (1,1,1) (3 credits)
MUSIC 210, 211, 212: Music History I, II, III ( Aut,Win,Spr ) (3,3,3) (9 credits)
MUSIC 303: Second-Year Theory III ( Autumn ) (3 credits)
MUSIC 306: Second-Year Ear Training III ( Autumn ) (1 credit)
At least 3 credits from among: MUSIC 250, MUSIC 251, MUSIC 252, or any 400-level Ethnomusicology course
TOTAL CORE CREDITS: 36
Additional Music Requirements
Approved upper-level Music Theory electives to be selected from MUSIC 472, 485, 487, and/or 489 (6 credits)
MUSIC 470: Analysis of Tonal Music: Introduction to Schenker (3 credits)
MUSIC 471: Introduction to Atonal Theory and Analysis (3 credits)
MUSICP 320-363: Vocal or Instrumental instruction (2, 2, 2, 2, 2) (10 credits)
Ensemble Requirements
Participation in at least one School of Music ensemble is required during every quarter of applied music instruction. (5 credits)
Strings : University Symphony Orchestra (MUSEN 300) is required druing every quarter of applied instruction.
Piano : Three quarters of Accompanying (MUSEN 325), and at least 1 credit of choral ensemble is required during every quarter of applied instruction.
Students must satisfy a piano proficiency requirement equivalent to passing MUSAP 135.
TOTAL MAJOR CREDITS: 66
Language Skills = 5 cr. English Composition; 5 cr. Foreign Language (third quarter)
Areas of Inquiry - 75 credits total (of which 15 cr. of major dept. prefix can count but not toward 90 non-Music)
A&H - Arts and Humanities = 20 cr.; SSc - Social Sciences = 20 cr.; NSc - Natural Sciences= 20 cr.
TOTAL NON-MUSIC CREDITS: 90
See the Degree Program Sheet below for a checklist of course requirements.
Bachelor of Arts - Music Theory Option Degree Program Sheet (PDF)

requirementCueLines: Bachelor of Arts - Music, Voice Option | School of Music | University of Washington
Bachelor of Arts - Music, Voice Option

This degree is offered with a major in Music, and is intended for students who wish to emphasize general competence in music within the framework of a liberal arts education. Students must complete a minimum of 180 credits, of which 90 must be taken in departments other than the School of Music, including the Language Skills , Reasoning and Writing in Context , and Areas of Inquiry requirements of the College of Arts and Sciences. Music majors are required to earn a minimum grade of 2.0 in each music course (core and elective) that is to count toward music degree requirements. A GPA of 2.5 in music is required for graduation.
MUSIC 113: Beginning Ear Training ( Autumn ) (1 credit)
MUSIC 119: Theory ( Autumn ) (3 credits)
MUSIC 120: Survey of Music History ( Aut/Win/Spr ) (5 credits) or proficiency exam
MUSIC 201, 202: First-Year Theory I, II ( Winter, Spring ) (3,3) (6 credits)
MUSIC 204, 205: First-Year Ear Training I, II ( Winter, Spring ) (1,1) (2 credits)
MUSIC 203, 301, 302: First-Year Theory III & Second-Year Theory I, II ( Aut,Win,Spr ) (3,3,3) (9 credits)
MUSIC 206, 304, 305: First-Year Ear Training III & Second Year-Ear Training I, II ( Aut,Win,Spr ) (1,1,1) (3 credits)
MUSIC 210, 211, 212: Music History I, II, III ( Aut,Win,Spr ) (3,3,3) (9 credits)
MUSIC 303: Second-Year Theory III ( Autumn ) (3 credits)
MUSIC 306: Second-Year Ear Training III ( Autumn ) (1 credit)
At least 3 credits from among: MUSIC 250, MUSIC 251, MUSIC 252, or any 400-level Ethnomusicology course
TOTAL CORE CREDITS: 36
Additional Music Requirements
Approved upper-level Music Theory or History electives (see list of course options) (6 credits)
MUSICP 320 Voice Lessons (18 credits)
MUSIC 307, 308, 309: Diction for Singers (2, 2, 2) (6 credits)
Ensemble Requirements
Participation in at least one School of Music ensemble is required during every quarter of applied music instruction. (6 credits)
Must include six quarters in a large conducted choral ensemble; MUSEN 350 (University Chorale), MUSEN 351 (Chamber Singers) or MUSEN 307 (Recital Choir).
Students must satisfy a piano proficiency requirement equivalent to passing MUSAP 135.
Foreign Language Requirements

Voice students must complete first-year language courses (through 103) in TWO languages (French, German, and/or Italian). These may also satisfy the UW foreign language requirement and count toward VLPA credits.
TOTAL MAJOR CREDITS: 72
Language Skills = 5 cr. English Composition; 5 cr. Foreign Language (third quarter)
Areas of Inquiry - 75 credits total (of which 15 cr. of major dept. prefix can count but not toward 90 non-Music)
A&H - Arts and Humanities = 20 cr.; SSc - Social Sciences = 20 cr.; NSc - Natural Sciences= 20 cr.
TOTAL NON-MUSIC CREDITS: 90
See the Degree Program Sheet below for a checklist of course requirements.
Bachelor of Arts - Voice Option Degree Program Sheet (PDF)
